commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=72b22e781273cd8f925d1d5f2543201c13005a4f)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds runtime and doctor filtering/quarantine for unreadable or non-normalizable active tool schemas, with regression tests for malformed tool schema handling.

PR surface: Source +257, Tests +333. Total +590 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current allowlist expansion reads bundled tool.name before the PR's bundled quarantine path runs, so a throwing bundled row can still crash that setup. I did not run tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Draft maintainer PR needs author/maintainer handling for the bundled allowlist repair and the compatibility scope decision, rather than a standalone ClawSweeper repair lane.

target.title: fix(agents): harden runtime tool schema diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary

- Quarantines active runtime tools whose input schema cannot safely reach provider/model runtime projection, instead of letting one bad tool crash assistant turn setup before content.
- Validates active tool schemas in doctor through the same provider-normalization plus runtime-projection path used by assistant turns.
- Pruned this PR back to the critical runtime/doctor slice: no OpenAI-strict inventory side work, no broad fixture-only churn, and no unrelated provider catalog changes.

## Linked context

Related #87141

This is the critical runtime/doctor slice from the larger fuzzing branch. Provider catalog isolation and Gateway MCP schema hardening stay separate.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: an unsupported, unreadable, or non-serializable active tool schema is filtered/quarantined before model session creation, and doctor can surface the blocker instead of crashing or silently skipping it.
- Real environment tested: final rebased local focused Vitest wrapper across runtime projection, doctor checks, quarantine logging, and live embedded attempt setup; AWS Crabbox changed gate on the pushed patch surface.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts`
  - `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
  - private-name/private-path touched-file scrub
  - `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
  - `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`
- Evidence after fix: final focused Vitest passed 3 routed shards / 79 tests at head `72b22e78127`; oxfmt passed; diff check passed; touched-file scrub found no private paths or private reported names; autoreview was clean; AWS Crabbox `run_f13eec88c992` on lease `cbx_d4b51d684986` ran `pnpm check:changed` with lanes `core` and `coreTests`, exiting 0.
- Observed result after fix: unreadable tool rows, unreadable names/parameters, and non-serializable parameter schemas are removed before provider normalization; provider-repairable raw schemas still reach provider normalization; post-normalization unsupported schemas are quarantined before model session creation; healthy sibling tools remain available.
- What was not tested: a live packaged third-party plugin. Coverage uses synthetic `fuzzplugin`/`mockplugin` fixtures and the same runtime projection path used by assistant setup.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (79 tests passed)
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- private-name/private-path touched-file scrub
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` (clean after fixing active-tool ordering findings)
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` (AWS Crabbox `run_f13eec88c992`, lease `cbx_d4b51d684986`, exit 0)

Regression coverage added:

- runtime projection filtering for unreadable tool descriptors and rows
- provider-normalizable prefiltering that preserves provider-repairable schemas
- explicit `tools.allow` handling for unreadable tool rows without crashing
- doctor runtime schema findings for unreadable active tool rows without throwing
- live embedded attempt quarantine after active-tool selection and before provider normalization/model session creation
- quarantine logging that does not re-read unreadable rows

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Bad active runtime tool schemas are reported/quarantined instead of crashing assistant turn setup.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Tool execution availability now fails around the bad active tool instead of failing the whole assistant turn.

What is the highest-risk area?

Runtime compatibility for plugins/tools with malformed schema metadata.

How is that risk mitigated?

The pre-normalization filter only runs after active tool selection, so disabled or policy-excluded tools do not emit blocked-tool telemetry. It removes unreadable rows/fields and non-serializable schemas; provider-repairable schemas still reach provider normalization, and the existing post-normalization runtime projection quarantines schemas the model runtime still cannot accept.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Draft PR for maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

GitHub CI and maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local autoreview findings on doctor unreadable-row handling, pre-normalization provider repair false positives, live attempt normalization ordering, disabled-tool quarantine telemetry, explicit allowlist row reads, and mutable normalization input were addressed before this push.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
